name: He gave them bread from heaven
notes: In John 6 the multitude, having been fed by the loaves, press Jesus for a sign and recall the manna, quoting Scripture: 'He gave them bread from heaven to eat.' Their citation gathers up the testimony of the Law and the Psalms to the wilderness provision. Psalm 78:24 rehearses how the LORD rained down manna and gave them of the corn of heaven, and Nehemiah 9:15 recounts that He gavest them bread from heaven for their hunger. Exodus 16:4 records the original word of the LORD, 'I will rain bread from heaven for you.' The shared core is the heaven-given bread that sustained Israel, which Jesus takes up to point beyond the manna to Himself as the true bread from heaven. Each account keeps its own vantage: Exodus the LORD's first promise, the Psalm and Nehemiah the worshipping remembrance, and John the people's appeal that opens onto the bread of life.
